original_hypothesis.title: ILOManager ownership can be mis-assigned at deployment due to tx.origin in constructor, enabling pre-initialize privileged actions
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: ILOManager constructor executes `transferOwnership(tx.origin)`. If deployed via a deployer contract or through complex automation, tx.origin may not be the intended owner (e.g., a relayer EOA), giving that EOA temporary ownership until initialize() is called. The temporary owner could call onlyOwner functions (setFeeTaker, setILOPoolImplementation, setDefaultDeadlineOffset, etc.) to alter system behavior before initialize transfers ownership to initialOwner.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a known deployment footgun that can create a narrow but real window for privileged misconfiguration. Even without a public mempool, operational deployment systems (relayers, factories, batchers) can produce unintended tx.origin values.
deployment_assumptions: ILOManager is deployed in a transaction where tx.origin != intended final owner and initialize() is not atomic with deployment
The temporary tx.origin owner can submit transactions before initialize() completes
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Deploy.s.sol deploys then initializes in the same broadcast script, but atomicity depends on whether these are separate transactions; scripts show separate blocks in code and thus likely separate txs.
preconditions: Deployment is not atomic with initialize() (separate transactions)
tx.origin is controlled by an entity other than intended owner (e.g., relayer)
Attacker has ability to send txs from that tx.origin-controlled EOA (or is that EOA)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Ownership is set in constructor using tx.origin rather than msg.sender or deferred to initialize.
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Between deployment tx and initialize tx

operational_questions: Are deployment and initialize executed in a single transaction or multiple?
Who controls tx.origin during deployment on the target chain (EOA, multisig module, relayer)?
validation_requirements: Reproduce deployment via the actual pipeline and inspect owner() between deployment and initialization
Attempt onlyOwner calls in between to confirm practical window

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Observe manager deployed but not yet initialized
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Use temporary ownership (tx.origin) to call onlyOwner setters (e.g., setFeeTaker)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Allow initialization to proceed, masking earlier configuration changes
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires being (or controlling) tx.origin EOA used in deployment

potential_operational_impact.administrative_compromise: Medium: temporary owner can alter key parameters affecting future pools
potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Medium: misconfiguration may impact fees, refund deadlines, or pool implementations
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium: can reset parameters if detected; damage may persist for already-created pools
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Global configuration and future pool deployments

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: onlyOwner config changes occurring before initialize() event/logs (if any)
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Unexpected owner address at deployment time

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
